content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

All seven amendments identify real defects in `materialize_beads` and `handle_export_beads`: cross-milestone title collisions via unfiltered `existing_by_title`, unguarded `sync_flush()` that can publish foreign unsynced mutations, fragile stdout token parsing for created bead IDs, non-idempotent comment posting (codified by the current test), workstream-specific title collisions, missing handling of implicit bead IDs in `depends_on`, and lack of a journal event for export operations. Each is a correctness, safety, or consistency bug backed by concrete code references.

## Votes

- `fr-1-34318126`: Accept
  Confirmed: `existing_by_title` at service.rs:1496-1504 is built from `BrCommand::list_all()` (repo-wide, including deferred/closed) and `materialize_bead_entry` (1669-1693) pops a match using only the normalized title — no milestone/type/status filter. Generic titles (`Core`, `Validation`) will rebind to unrelated beads and rewire them under the new root hierarchy. Remediation to require a milestone-scoped marker plus rejection of closed/type-mismatched matches is sound.
- `fr-1-d3e593a6`: Accept
  Confirmed: service.rs:1647-1650 calls unconditional `sync_flush()`, whose implementation at br_process.rs:1423-1456 runs `sync_flush_locked` with no ownership/health guard and clears all pending mutation state on success. Since `handle_export_beads` builds a fresh `BrMutationAdapter` (milestone.rs:370-371), any preexisting foreign entries under `.beads/.br-unsynced-mutations.d/` will be published. The codebase already exposes `sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy` (br_process.rs:1562) for exactly this guarantee.
- `fr-1-333b58da`: Accept
  Confirmed: `resolve_exported_bead_id` (1712-1739) and `candidate_bead_ids_from_create_stdout` (3237-3255) pick the last token-like suffix from stdout without verifying it against the created bead's title. CLI stdout changes (e.g. trailing quoted title or future formatting) could silently produce the wrong ID, attaching dependencies/comments to an unrelated bead. The fix — hint-then-verify via `br show`/title match — is the standard pattern already used elsewhere in this file.
- `fr-1-08a2a084`: Accept
  Confirmed: workstream description comment (1552-1563) and planning rationale comment (1591-1604) are posted on every call with no `created` gate. The idempotency test at 15128-15229 actually accepts 8 fresh `comment added` mutations even with `created_beads == 0`, codifying the defect. This directly violates the stated idempotency acceptance criterion and will accumulate duplicate rationale comments on every rerun. Gate on `created == true` (or diff against existing comments) and tighten the test.
- `fr-1-03293bf5`: Accept
  Confirmed and complementary to fr-1-34318126. The root is created with label `milestone-root` (1521) and workstream epics with no labels at all (1536); `materialize_bead_entry` never filters by labels, so labels provide no protection against collision. Two milestones containing a workstream named `Core` or `Validation` — or sharing any root name — will rebind across milestones. Adding a `milestone:<id>` label and filtering `existing_by_title` to entries carrying it is a minimal, correct mitigation.
- `fr-1-9c45d658`: Accept
  Confirmed: `proposal_id_map` at service.rs:1606-1608 registers only when `proposal.bead_id.is_some()`, but `MilestoneBundle::validate` in Compatibility mode (bundle.rs:91-410) accepts proposals without an explicit `bead_id` and generates implicit IDs solely for local validation (they are not written back to the bundle). A valid bundle with implicit-ID-based `depends_on` references will fail partway through `materialize_beads` (after partial creation + no flush). Registering the canonical implicit id in the map and normalizing each `depends_on` via `normalize_bead_reference` closes the gap.
- `fr-1-988679fb`: Accept
  Confirmed: `handle_export_beads` at milestone.rs:360-380 mutates `br` state (creates beads, adds comments, links deps) and sets the active milestone without appending any `MilestoneJournalEvent`. Other mutating milestone commands maintain a journal trail, so this is both a consistency gap and a forensic hole — partial/failed exports leave no first-class record of which root/workstream/task IDs were produced or which bundle hash shipped. A `BeadsExported` event with the bundle hash and `BeadMaterializationReport` is the right fix.
